title: Add Remote Endpoint to CoilsChangedEventArgs and RegistersChangedEventArgs
author: oollie34
body:
ModbusTcpServer raises CoilsChanged and RegistersChanged events, but CoilsChangedEventArgs and RegistersChangedEventArgs only expose UnitIdentifier and the changed coil/register addresses.

For Modbus TCP server use cases with multiple clients using the same unit id and address map, it would be nice to know which TCP client performed the write. 

It should be simple to include the remote endpoint in events.

title: Add support for 16 KB page sizes for libraries to support Maui -> Android 15+ builds
author: MartBentNedap
body:
## Description

All underlying libraries which are used by FluentModbus should support 16 KB page sizes. This is to accommodate the Android 16 update. 

Here is the warning output when a Maui app is compiled with a library that does not support 16 KB page sizes. In this case this is IO Ports, which is used in the FluentModbus library.

` /usr/local/share/dotnet/packs/Microsoft.Android.Sdk.Darwin/35.0.101/tools/Xamarin.Android.Common.targets(2132,3): warning XA0141: Android 16 will require 16 KB page sizes, shared library 'libSystem.IO.Ports.Native.so' does not have a 16 KB page size. Please inform the authors of the NuGet package 'runtime.linux-arm64.runtime.native.System.IO.Ports' version '5.0.0-rtm.20519.4' which contains 'runtimes/linux-arm64/native/libSystem.IO.Ports.Native.so'. See https://developer.android.com/guide/practices/page-sizes for more details. `

Google has already issued a warning in their console indicating that apps that do not support this will no longer by able to upload apps to the Google Play Store. While we can temporarily drop support, we do need to add the support back in the near future.

## References

More info about the page sizes can be found here: 

https://developer.android.com/guide/practices/page-sizes
https://android-developers.googleblog.com/2025/05/prepare-play-apps-for-devices-with-16kb-page-size.html

title: Issue on the server with rejected requests and timed out requests
author: Eastwo00d
body:
Hi, 

I think there is a problem concerning the management of refused tcp requests. When I start a modbus server on a specific IP and a specific Unit Identifier, if I use several MODBUS clients to request the same IP but on other unit IDs (that are not handled by the server), the CPU usage skyrockets till the server is not able to respond to requests he is supposed to...

Also, when I am debugging my program, if I stay long enough in a breakpoint so the tcp timeout is reached, the server refuses all the requests after resuming the program. Stopping and restarting the server solves this problem.

Regards

title: Transaction Identifier not checked in ModbusTcpClient, resulting in incorrect data
author: LanderV-Vintecc
body:
The Transaction Identifier (the field identifying each modbus transaction) is ignored in ModbusTcpClient and ModbusTcpClientAsync:

https://github.com/Apollo3zehn/FluentModbus/blob/bbd7dcd1d628811a73328233700ca352b2d3d71c/src/FluentModbus/Client/ModbusTcpClient.cs#L294

https://github.com/Apollo3zehn/FluentModbus/blob/bbd7dcd1d628811a73328233700ca352b2d3d71c/src/FluentModbus/Client/ModbusTcpClientAsync.cs#L98

According to the official docs, it is necessary to check it though: https://www.modbus.org/docs/Modbus_Messaging_Implementation_Guide_V1_0b.pdf (page 10):

> Several MODBUS transactions can be activated simultaneously on the same TCP Connection.
Remark: If this is done then the MODBUS transaction identifier must be used to uniquely identify the matching requests and responses.

A real-life example:

![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/6532446f-794d-41e2-8999-105b44f05756)

A read request is written with transaction identifier 10.
The following message is a response with transaction identifier 0F.
Then, we get the response with transaction identifier 10.

Ignoring the transaction identifier will result in an incorrect value.

title: Serial Port not found
author: MassimilianoScarpetta
body:
Hi @Apollo3zehn!

First of all thank you for your awesome work, this library is very performant and easy to use.
Anyway, this time, I got an issue while trying to connect to a serial port.

I'm using a nport 5110 device that converts ethernet-RS485 signals. It also maps and extends serial ports.
I'm using your library to try to connect to the serial port that the nport device created.
Unfortunately the library don't find the serial port. I've tried to use ModbusPoll to connect to the port and it worked.

Could you please tell me if I misunderstood something or if there can be a workaround to solve this?

title: ModbusRtuServer Start functions handle IModbusRtuSerialPort inconsistently
author: Bertimus-maximus
body:
Hello,

I think that when a ModbusRtuServer is started with an IModbusRtuSerialPort instead of a string, the _serialPort object is not getting set.  In my testing this lead to not being able to use IsConnected.

I think the fix is to move `serialPort = serialPort;` into the start function that takes the interface as its argument.

Thanks.

title: Is it possible to set the high bit in front or the low bit

title: Inconsistency in WriteMultipleCoilsAsync Function Signature
author: kaheimri
body:
Hi.
First of all, many thanks for providing and maintaining this library in your spare time.

### Description

I encountered an issue while trying to use the `WriteMultipleCoilsAsync` function in `async` code. The function is supposed to return a `Task`, but it appears to have a `void` return type in both the official documentation and the decompiled sources.

### Steps to Reproduce

Attempt to use the `WriteMultipleCoilsAsync` function in an `async` context.

### Expected Behavior

The `WriteMultipleCoilsAsync` function should be awaitable.

### Actual Behavior

Attempting to await this function results in the following compile error:
```
error CS4008: Cannot await 'void'
```

### Additional Information

- GitHub Source Code: The source code on GitHub clearly shows a return value of Task, see [WriteMultipleCoilsAsync](https://github.com/Apollo3zehn/FluentModbus/blob/dev/src/FluentModbus/Client/ModbusClientAsync.cs#L352).
- Official Documentation: The official documentation also states a void return type, see [WriteMultipleCoilsAsync API](https://apollo3zehn.github.io/FluentModbus/api/FluentModbus.ModbusClient.html#FluentModbus_ModbusClient_WriteMultipleCoilsAsync_System_Int32_System_Int32_System_Boolean___System_Threading_CancellationToken_).
- Decompiled Source: The decompiled source using dotPeek shows a void return type.
    ```c#
    public void WriteMultipleCoilsAsync(int unitIdentifier, int startingAddress, bool[] values, CancellationToken cancellationToken = default)
    ```
- Git Blame: A git blame on the code does not reveal any changes from `void` to `Task` return value.

### Attachments

I have attached a small demo project that demonstrates the issue. [fluentmdbus.zip](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/18285454/fluentmdbus.zip)

### Conclusion

There seems to be a discrepancy between the GitHub source code and the actual compiled library. This inconsistency is causing issues when trying to use the function in `async` code.

title: Can working on ModbusRtu with tcp server?

body:
The sensor device working as modbusrtu server，connected to a gateway.
Gateway working as tcp client for transparent transmission，and will  initiatively connect to the specific tcp server.

I want to know how to use FluentModbus create a tcp server to be connected by the gateway tcp client and communicate for modbusrtu?

title: `ModbusTcpClient .Initialize` throws `Exception` -> hard to catch / determine what went wrong
author: JaggerJo
body:
Hey @Apollo3zehn 👋

First, great library - thanks for maintaining.

We are having a small problem with `Initialize` on the `ModbusTcpClient`. We want to periodically retry to connect if connecting fails. We could do that by wrapping the call to initialise in a try catch, the issue is that we'd need to catch all exceptions. 

https://github.com/Apollo3zehn/FluentModbus/blob/bbd7dcd1d628811a73328233700ca352b2d3d71c/src/FluentModbus/Client/ModbusTcpClient.cs#L170-L171

It would be nice to throw a special kind of exception here so that we can only catch e.g. `FluentModbusException` instead of `Exception`.

What do you think?

Are we completely missing something here?

title: Exception "There is no valid request available" when any client reconnect.
author: eryk955
body:
Hi
I have big problem with creating ModbusTCP Server. Server works, data flows, but when client reconnect (like PLC, because don't like to keep alive connection) FluentModbus call exception "System.Exception" and "There is no valid request available.". So in short, exception occurs, when any client tried to reconnect.

I checked this also on Node-Red ModbusTCP Client. Init connection is ok, but when Connected, VS call exception like above. I cannot find reason why this happens, so please, help :)

title: Pass new value register value on write function codes to request validator
author: AthenaAzuraeaX
body:
The request validation function today takes Unit identifier, Function code, Address and Quantity of registers.

Do you think it would be possible to also pass the new register value from a write function code? That would allow us to validate the incoming data and possibly return Illegal Data Value exception code if it fails.

title: Not possible to write to address > 65535
author: simon-baer
body: I need to write to a holding register with an address > 65535. I don't known if this is an extension of the Modbus protocol, other Modbus clients are able to do this. It would be great if you could support this too!

title: Serial I/O
author: SY6502
body:
I found "ReadTimeout { get; set; } = 1000;" in ModbusRtuClient.cs. Is this 1s timeout the best value? I read in some device's Modbus docs that max timeout for response is 5 seconds. I am not sure is this the Modbus standard or specific timeout for this device.

I also see that default baud rate is 9600. Is this recommended speed? If devices support 115200, is there reason not to use it?

And one recommendation - it may be good to have method like RawRW. Instead of ReadHoldingRegisters or WriteSingleCoil, its parameters include byte array of whole command (without CRC for RTU). unitIdentifier can be included in command or sent as separate parameter.

title: Linux / IsConnected
author: Steve0212a
body:
I have code that is shared between Windows and Linux that uses the FluentModbus client (synchronous) to talk to Modbus devices.  I am currently testing failures such as the Modbus device is turned off or disconnected from the network.  

On Windows, the code works perfectly - meaning that the IsConnected flag goes to false after having previously been true from before I turned the device off.

However, on Linux, it establishes the connection and the IsConnected flag is true.  I then turn the device off, but the the IsConnected flag never goes to false.

Any thoughts on how to fix or work around this?
